activities_projects.title: Quantitative Analysis of $H_2O$ Electrolysis
activities_projects.objective: Mathematically verify the molar ratio of gases evolved during electrolysis
materials: Hoffman voltameter or DIY electrolysis kit
steps: Perform electrolysis for exactly 10 minutes at a constant voltage
Measure the exact volume (in mL) of gas collected at the cathode and anode
Calculate the molar ratio using the Ideal Gas Law approximation ($V \propto n$)
Explain deviations from the theoretical $2:1$ ratio (e.g., solubility of $O_2$ in water)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes stoichiometry and potential experimental errors in gas collection
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision of volume measurements and depth of error analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Kinetics of Reaction: Temperature vs. Rate
activities_projects.objective: Investigate how temperature affects the rate of a chemical reaction
steps: Place the flask over the 'X' and mix $Na_2S_2O_3$ and $HCl$
Time how long it takes for the 'X' to disappear due to sulphur precipitation
Repeat the experiment at $30^\circ C, 40^\circ C, 	ext{ and } 50^\circ C$
Plot a graph of $1/	ext{time}$ (rate) vs. Temperature
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates the relationship between thermal energy and molecular collisions
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the rate-temperature graph and trend interpretation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Redox Titration: Vitamin C Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Use redox reactions to quantify the concentration of an antioxidant
steps: Prepare a known concentration of Vitamin C solution
Add starch indicator; it turns blue-black when free iodine is present
Titrate iodine into the Vitamin C until a permanent blue-black color appears
Calculate the mass of Vitamin C reacted based on the volume of iodine used
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies redox principles to quantitative chemical analysis
activities_projects.evaluation: Calculations showing the stoichiometric relationship between Ascorbic acid and Iodine (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Thermite Reaction Research & Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Analyze high-energy displacement reactions and their industrial utility
steps: Study the reaction: $Fe_2O_3 + 2Al ightarrow Al_2O_3 + 2Fe + 	ext{Heat}$
Calculate the enthalpy change (theoretical) using Bond Energy data
Research why this reaction is used for welding railway tracks
Design a safety protocol for handling such highly exothermic reactions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the thermodynamic feasibility and industrial application of displacement reactions
activities_projects.evaluation: Comprehensive report on enthalpy and industrial safety standards (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Amphoteric Nature Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Identify substances that react with both acids and bases
steps: React Zinc with $HCl$ and confirm the gas evolved
React Zinc with concentrated $NaOH$ and confirm the gas evolved
Write balanced equations for both, identifying the 'Zincate' ion
Compare these results with a standard metal-acid reaction
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes knowledge of complex ion formation in redox/displacement contexts
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct chemical equations for both acidic and basic environments (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Law of Definite Proportions Verification
activities_projects.objective: Prove that a chemical compound always contains exactly the same proportion of elements by mass
steps: Weigh a sample of $CuCO_3$ and heat it to decompose into $CuO$
Calculate the mass of $CuO$ formed
Repeat with a different starting mass of $CuCO_3$
Calculate the ratio of $	ext{Mass of } CuO : 	ext{Mass of } CuCO_3$ for both trials
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Validates the fundamental laws of chemical combination through decomposition
activities_projects.evaluation: Consistency of the mass ratios across multiple trials (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Determining Water of Crystallization
activities_projects.objective: Quantify the moles of water associated with a salt crystal
steps: Weight the blue crystals accurately
Heat until the salt turns completely white (anhydrous)
Reweigh the anhydrous salt
Calculate the mass of water lost and determine the value of '$x$' in $CuSO_4 \cdot xH_2O$
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Uses mass-mass relationship to determine chemical formulas
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the calculated molar ratio of water to salt (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Blue Bottle Reaction (Reversible Redox)
activities_projects.objective: Observe a complex oscillating/reversible redox system
steps: Prepare a solution of glucose and $NaOH$ with a few drops of Methylene blue
Observe the solution turn colorless upon standing (Reduction)
Shake the flask to introduce Oxygen and watch it turn blue (Oxidation)
Explain the cycle in terms of electron transfer
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the dynamic nature of reversible oxidation-reduction reactions
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of the role of Atmospheric Oxygen as an oxidizing agent (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Corrosion Cell Construction
activities_projects.objective: Model the electrochemical nature of rusting
steps: Wrap a copper wire around one part of an iron nail
Place it in the indicator gel
Observe the blue (anodic) and pink (cathodic) regions
Identify where oxidation and reduction are occurring specifically
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Conceptualizes corrosion as a micro-electrochemical cell reaction
activities_projects.evaluation: Identifying the anode/cathode and interpreting color changes (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Stoichiometry of Precipitation
activities_projects.objective: Predict the mass of a precipitate using balanced equations
steps: React a precisely weighed amount of Lead Nitrate with excess Potassium Iodide
Filter, wash, and dry the precipitate ($PbI_2$)
Weigh the dry precipitate
Compare the 'Experimental Yield' with the 'Theoretical Yield' calculated from the equation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Mastery of limiting reagents and percentage yield calculations
activities_projects.evaluation: Calculation of percentage yield and explanation of mass loss (10 marks)
